content:
First we show that the sum of the first 2n elements of M is 
   S(2n)=∑_{k=1}^{2n}M(k)=3n². 
Indeed from the closed‐form  
   M(k)=k+⌊(k−1)/2⌋  
one finds by direct summation that S(2n)=3n².  Hence if this sum is 300,  
   3n²=300  ⇒  n²=100  ⇒  n=10.  
Answer: \boxed{10}
